channel.title: Green Curve Insights
channel.description: Daily ESG and climate compliance intelligence for Indian businesses.

item.title: Sustainability as a Growth Driver: What EY's Global Read Means for Indian CFOs Navigating BRSR, Credit Markets, and Supply Chain Scrutiny
item.description: EY's Global Climate Change and Sustainability Services Leader Alexis Gazzo, writing in ESG Today (https://www.esgtoday.com/sustainability-as-a-driver-for-growth/), argues that sustainability has decisively crossed from compliance territory into commercial competitiveness and capital access — driven

item.title: SBTi's 2026–2030 Strategy Shift: From Target Validation to Implementation Support — What It Means for India's 10,000+ BRSR-Reporting Companies
item.description: The Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) has announced a fundamental strategic pivot for 2026–2030: moving from its decade-long focus on target-setting and validation toward active implementation support, sector-specific standards, and geographic expansion — with high-emitting regions, including

item.title: U.S. State AGs Sue ISS Over ESG Proxy Advice: What the Anti-ESG Legal Offensive Means for Indian Institutional Investors, BRSR Disclosures, and Cross-Border Capital
item.description: Four U.S. state Attorneys General — Texas, Nebraska, Iowa, and West Virginia — have filed coordinated lawsuits against proxy advisory giant Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS), alleging consumer protection violations, conflicts of interest, and ideologically motivated investment advice disguise

item.title: Singapore–World Bank Carbon Markets Programme: What India's Carbon Market Architects and BRSR-Reporting Companies Must Watch
item.description: Singapore's National Climate Change Secretariat and the World Bank Group have jointly launched the Singapore Carbon Markets Programme, a structured initiative to build national carbon market infrastructure — registries, MRV systems, and institutional capacity — across developing economies, with expl

item.title: CIP's €1.5 Billion Biomethane Fund: What Europe's Bioenergy Capital Surge Means for India's Hard-to-Abate Sectors, BRSR Disclosures, and Green Gas Strategy
item.description: Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (CIP) has launched Advanced Bioenergy Fund II (ABF II), targeting €1.5 billion to scale industrial biomethane production across Europe — anchored by a €200 million commitment from the European Investment Fund under the EU's InvestEU and REPowerEU mandates. Reportin

item.title: Google's 24/7 CFE Commitment Holds: What the GHG Protocol Hourly Matching Debate Means for Indian Data Centre Operators, RE100 Signatories, and BRSR Scope 2 Disclosures
item.description: Google's Singapore and Malaysia Managing Director Ben King confirmed at the Ecosperity conference that the company's 2030 24/7 Carbon-Free Energy (CFE) commitment — hourly, local matching of electricity consumption with clean energy supply — remains fully intact despite a $190 billion AI infrastruct

item.title: La Caisse's Novisto Investment Signals Institutional Capital Flowing Into ESG Data Infrastructure — What Indian CFOs Must Read Into This
item.description: Quebec's La Caisse — one of the world's largest institutional investors managing over CAD 450 billion — has made a strategic investment in Novisto, a Montreal-based ESG data management and reporting software platform, explicitly citing CSRD compliance pressure and California's climate disclosure law

item.title: Pentagreen's $800M GIP Close: What the FAST-P Blended Finance Architecture Means for India's Transition Infrastructure Capital Gap
item.description: Pentagreen Capital — the HSBC and Temasek-backed debt financing platform — has reached an $800 million second close for its Green Investments Partnership (GIP), a blended finance vehicle operating under Singapore's MAS-anchored Financing Asia's Transition Partnership (FAST-P). Critically for Indian

item.title: Novata's Risk Atlas: Why AI-Powered Supply Chain Risk Monitoring for Private Markets Has Direct Implications for Indian PE-Backed Companies and BRSR Value Chain Disclosures
item.description: Novata's launch of Risk Atlas — an AI-powered, multi-category ESG risk monitoring tool for private markets portfolios and supply chains — signals a structural shift in how global GPs and LPs will assess, screen, and engage with portfolio companies on ESG risk, including those domiciled in India. For

item.title: Datamaran Core's CSRD/ISSB Enhancements: What AI-Driven Double Materiality and IRO Gap Analysis Mean for Indian Companies Navigating BRSR Core and Global Disclosure Convergence
item.description: Datamaran's May 2026 enhancements to its AI-powered Datamaran Core platform — including a Double Materiality IRO gap analysis benchmarked against audited CSRD peer reports, dynamic stakeholder intelligence, and AI-generated disclosure recommendations — represent a material shift in how technology is

item.title: CPCB Extends Interim Relief for Importers of 85 EEE Items: EPR Registration Deadline Now 30 June 2023
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has issued a clarificatory letter dated 9 May 2023 to the Customs authorities, extending interim arrangements for releasing imported consignments of producers of 85 categories of Electrical and Electronic Equipment (EEE) under the E-Waste (Management) Rules

item.title: CPCB Greenlights Customs Release for 85 EEE Item Importers: Undertaking-Based Clearance Under E-Waste Rules 2022
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has written to the Central Board of Excise and Customs allowing the release of imported consignments of 85 categories of Electrical and Electronic Equipment (EEE) covered under the new E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, subject to an undertaking from the Pro

item.title: Plastic Waste Management (2nd Amendment) Rules 2022: EPR Tightens for Producers, Importers & Brand Owners
item.description: On 6 July 2022, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) notified the Plastic Waste Management (Second Amendment) Rules, 2022, significantly strengthening the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework for plastic packaging. The amendment introduces clear definitions for

item.title: Guidelines on Extended Producer Responsibility for Plastic Packaging
item.description: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has notified the Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2022, introducing legally binding Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) guidelines for plastic packaging. Producers, Importers, and Brand Owners (PIBOs) are now accountable for the en

item.title: ESG Index Inclusion as a Capital Strategy: What Indian CFOs and CSOs Must Understand About MSCI, FTSE4Good, and the $500M Demand Signal
item.description: Inclusion in major ESG indexes — MSCI ESG Leaders, FTSE4Good, S&P ESG — is no longer merely a reputational marker; it is a direct mechanism for generating incremental institutional demand for a company's equity, with documented examples of single index additions triggering $500 million in net inflow

item.title: Australia Raises Sustainability Reporting Thresholds: What India's BRSR Architecture and Listed Companies Should Read Into This Global Rollback Signal
item.description: Australia's 2026 Budget has proposed doubling the revenue and asset thresholds for mandatory sustainability reporting, effectively removing a significant cohort of smaller companies from its climate disclosure regime — a move framed explicitly as a A$10.2 billion per year regulatory burden reduction

item.title: Crew Carbon's $25M Raise Signals a Convergence of Wastewater Infrastructure and Carbon Markets — What Indian Utilities, BRSR Reporters, and Carbon Credit Buyers Must Understand Now
item.description: Crew Carbon, a Yale University spinout, has closed a $25 million Series A to scale its wastewater-integrated enhanced mineral weathering technology — a carbon removal pathway that generates verified, permanent CO2 removal credits sold to corporate buyers including JPMorganChase, Google, and Stripe,

item.title: HSBC's $4 Billion China Cleantech Facility: What India's CFOs and CSOs Must Understand About the Capital Race They Are Now In
item.description: HSBC's $4 billion Sustainability and Transition Credit Facility — announced on 18 May 2026 and reported by ESG Today (https://www.esgtoday.com/hsbc-launches-4-billion-facility-to-help-china-clean-tech-companies-scale-internationally/) — is designed to accelerate the international scaling of Mainland

item.title: BBVA's 99% Renewable Electricity and 83% Scope 1&2 Reduction: The Operational Decarbonisation Benchmark Indian Banks Cannot Ignore
item.description: Spanish banking group BBVA has disclosed 99% renewable electricity coverage across its global operations in 2025 and an 83% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 emissions since 2019 — a performance benchmark that directly pressures Indian listed banks and financial institutions now subject to mandatory BRSR C

item.title: Draft Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2022: Tightening EPR and Expanding Producer Accountability
item.description: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) has published the Draft Plastic Waste Management (Third Amendment) Rules, 2021 — building on the EPR framework already issued in October 2021. The amendments sharpen definitions around plastic packaging, biodegradable plastics, recycler
